summary: Singapore's transition is led by the Cyber Security Agency, which in October 2025 opened a public consultation, running to the end of December 2025, on a Quantum-Safe Handbook, a Quantum Readiness Index and a draft Quantum-Safe Migration Handbook, and which states through its Committee of Supply 2026 position that post-quantum cryptography will be the mainstream solution for quantum-safe migration. The Monetary Authority of Singapore drives financial-sector work through an advisory that references the NIST standardisation process, joint experiments, sandbox reports and quantum funding, while the Infocomm Media Development Authority runs the National Quantum-Safe Network, launched in 2023 as Southeast Asia's first quantum-safe network infrastructure, and is consulting on draft reference specifications that combine post-quantum cryptography with quantum key distribution. All of these instruments remain advisory or in draft, so no organisation in Singapore is yet legally required to migrate.
processParticipation: NIST PQC standardisation process | referenced by the MAS quantum advisory and followed via Post-Quantum Cryptography Coalition roadmap categories in the CSA draft migration handbook
migrationTimeline:
2023 | IMDA launches the National Quantum-Safe Network Plus, Southeast Asia's first quantum-safe network infrastructure
2025 | CSA opens public consultation (23 Oct to 31 Dec 2025) on the Quantum-Safe Handbook, Quantum Readiness Index and draft Quantum-Safe Migration Handbook
2026 | Singapore government states post-quantum cryptography will be the mainstream solution for quantum-safe migration (Committee of Supply 2026); IMDA issues a draft reference specification requiring post-quantum cryptographic techniques on the QKD module to key manager link
govActors:
CSA | Cyber Security Agency of Singapore, national cyber authority leading the Quantum-Safe Handbook, Quantum Readiness Index and draft Quantum-Safe Migration Handbook and the position that PQC will be the mainstream quantum-safe solution
MAS | Monetary Authority of Singapore, financial regulator issuing the quantum advisory referencing the NIST PQC process and running sandbox experiments and quantum funding for the financial sector
IMDA | Infocomm Media Development Authority, operating the National Quantum-Safe Network Plus and issuing draft reference specifications combining post-quantum cryptography with quantum key distribution
mainRegulation:
CSA Quantum-Safe Handbook and Quantum Readiness Index (draft, public consultation 23 Oct to 31 Dec 2025) | national | guidance
CSA Quantum-Safe Migration Handbook (draft for public consultation, Oct 2025) | national | guidance
MAS Advisory on Quantum Computing and Post-Quantum Cryptography | national | guidance
IMDA Reference Specification: Secure Deployment of Quantum Key Distribution Networks (draft for public comment) | national | soft-law
IMDA Reference Specification: Quantum Key Distribution Networks (Issue 1, draft for public comment) | national | soft-law
obligation: No organisation in Singapore is legally required to adopt post-quantum cryptography. The CSA Quantum-Safe Handbook advises critical information infrastructure owners and government agencies to inventory cryptographic assets and plan migration to the NIST post-quantum baseline, and the MAS advisory makes the same recommendations to financial institutions, but both are advisory only and carry no enforceable obligation.
classificationBasis:
summary + govActors (CSA): Quantum-Safe Handbook and Quantum Readiness Index row, Committee of Supply 2026 speech (PQC the mainstream solution) row, and draft Quantum-Safe Migration Handbook row
dominantProcess NIST: MAS advisory references NIST's global standardisation process for PQC; CSA draft handbook references PQC roadmap categories from the Post-Quantum Cryptography Coalition. No Singaporean sovereign algorithm scheme stated, so NIST assigned. Note strong parallel QKD emphasis (IMDA), but the algorithm camp is NIST - REVIEW
MAS role: MAS advisory, Banque de France joint experiment, sandbox reports, FSTI Quantum track and S$100 million commitment rows
IMDA role: National Quantum-Safe Network, QKD reference specifications (combining PQC and QKD), and Quantum-Safe Singapore fact sheet rows
standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED, no specific FIPS or algorithm names in the included summaries
migrationTimeline / targetCompletion: OMITTED, no dated migration milestones or end date stated in the included summaries
